steps.rationale: Differentiate outer (sin -> cos) and inner (3x^2 + 1 -> 6x) separately.
Chain rule: multiply outer derivative by inner derivative.

line_results.note: Got dy/du correct but did not compute du/dx.

line_results.explanation_for_intervention: Student wrote the outer derivative cos(u) but skipped multiplying by du/dx = 6x. The substitution was set up correctly, so the gap is at the chain-rule combine step, not the inner-derivative computation itself.

intervention_recommendation.rationale: 9th occurrence in 4 consecutive sessions on the trig-outer specialization. Nudges have not produced self-correction. Time to walk through the multiply step explicitly, then have the student redo a similar problem.
intervention_recommendation.suggested_utterance_seed: Hold up — you set u = 3x^2 + 1 perfectly, but the chain rule needs you to multiply by du/dx. What's the derivative of 3x^2 + 1?
